comment.body: Ill post some things for next week that will help clear it up. Here is the gamma profile for MM's next week (i use volland until i can figure out a way to code it myself). That negative call gamma is your spot up vol up scenario as MM's buy back their deltas. The second photo is CHARM. Because we are in an OPEX week it is stronger. so it is compressing us preventing spot up vol up. Last photo is vanna. Negative vanna creates a resistance if its above us as VOL DECREASES which further prevents us rallying. Thats why im saying its going to be about how those puts get closed out

comment.body:
I get that things change daily, but she tends to conflate “intraday” posts with swing trading positions. If she talks about a JPM collar which will decay volatility as price moves down to 4700 towards expiration that is over 2 weeks away, and mentions a “breaking effect” can be felt around 5114.24, then that to me is consequential outside of just yesterday’s intraday price action. It clearly has implications on her March OPEX rally take, especially when she stated that price would oscillate in a more and more contracting channel. And that spot up would equate vol-up. 

And we closed far below the 5565 level yesterday, which should’ve been a concern considering this. And would actually support her thesis of shorting vol as it could be argued that we should go lower given the close and the environment we were in.

Instead today, we gap up, and rally hard, closing at highs of the day while volatility decays. And come to find, Alma was buying APPL and other calls below/at 5530 before close yesterday, along with long futures contracts.

You’d think she’d continue shorting volatility given the close. Which would mean she’d expect spot to come down further because price closed below the JPM strike at 5565.  And if her recommendation was to short vol yesterday, then why would she be buying calls? Because that would be contradictory to her “current tactic”, as price would have to continue to move down under 5565 in order to bring volatility down, according to her post yesterday.

She didn’t take any new positions today (at least this morning). She was riding ones she set up yesterday. So her analysis this morning was inconsequential to her current position for her March OPEX bounce.

So again, not very clear here. It certainly seemed obvious to me that we were due for a bounce. I even mentioned that was my expectation on Monday/Tuesday. But I was trying to stay honest towards Alma’s commentary yesterday, which obviously didn’t work out.

comment.body:
I think i understand where the confusion is. Ill try to break it down
1.) Volatility is down 8% but she said it would be up today.
VIX is a measure of 30-day ATM IV. OTM IV on 0DTE is a lot higher. For vol to go up on a longer basis we need to see an unwinding of puts and thats whats throwing everyone off. The short squeeze is getting stunted by 0DTE's (imo) and that isn't easy to see pre-market. Her posts are based on pre-market positioning.
2.) a 120 rip is 1.69% and it is higher, correct. But for you to make money your effectively betting that RV will exceed that. It could, but doing that on a 0DTE is fucking difficult because as the day goes on the prob ITM goes to 0. What if that rip comes at 4:20? Would you have held? etc etc
3.) This is more trading intraday vs longer term. She isn't shorting the Vix, she's probably shorting 1DTE PUTS. Ya it could've gone against her and she would've been blown out but the odds here aren't favoring downside moves.

lmk if this helps, i understand you're trying 
to learn. I do not trade 0DTE because i can't process volatility in realtime as fast lol

comment.body: Your chasing her trades instead of looking at the setup of WHY she is taking that trade. below the JPM collar we get vol compression towards the lower strike, that is vanna at work. The braking effect (imo) is more of a statement on where we would stop if we would keep going down. This is the 10th worst correction of all time, or something like that, due to the speed in which it happened. So what happened today? There was some japan news that pushed futures up. Asia rallied and so we opened above the JPM strike, that changes the dynamics at play and then becomes supportive.

comment.body: The tactic isn't "we go down in an oscillating pattern until april". It is oscillating like that because it is a controlled selloff (look at 2022) and not a panic selloff (as seen in SPX/vix beta). As you said, it was obvious there would be a bounce...so why wouldn't you be buying some calls here? Was it because you were concerned on a IV crush? RV is compressing here, short vol funds are going to start pushing the market higher if its enough to overpower the hedging dealer dynamics

comment.body:
I’m not chasing any trades. I’m taking into account her commentary honestly and literally.

Why would she go against her own commentary and buy calls/futures before close yesterday when we were in a spot down/vol down regime? And when spot up would equate to vol-up and destroy her “current tactic” of shorting vol? Did she have a crystal ball that Asia would rally? And did she know that spot up would actually lead to vol down and more liquidity? 

Clearly her taking long calls and futures yesterday before close was contradictory to her analysis. I still haven’t heard a reason as to how it’s not, based off what she wrote yesterday.

I didn’t buy yesterday because a very smart quant was stating she was shorting vol, and that spot up would equate vol up… which would mean if I bought calls and shares, I would be trading against, apparently, one of the best quants there is. Why would I do that?

comment.body:
I understand your frustration, because I was confused by the same shit lmao. I would like to point out 2 things

1) she is looking at FAR more data than what she’s posting. She’s also in positions in her portfolio we’re not in and doesn’t speak on. You have to keep that in mind 

2). With the data she does post, you need to use that and look at your own methodology and combine the 2. She has said, greeks are not everything, MM flows are not everything. You need to put it together with other tools. Even she uses TV indicators haha. 

This is the hard part of the learning, and trust me, I KNOW it’s fucking frustrating, can’t tell you how many hours I’ve spent just learning what the fuck MM GEX is among other things. This is gonna be a long journey, not something that’ll likely happen in a couple months or even a couple years. I’m taking it and applying it to what I already am using (I use a lot of math related models already but geared toward Frequency/Amplitude modulation and wavelet convolution). I think you will see more success there, and take it one bite at a time. I’m by no means an expert but I am seeing progress which is all I care about right now. My $0.02 to you

comment.body:
Well you think wrong. Not everyone got it. Look back at the thread after she posted her intraday revision. Lots of people were unclear as what to expect or how to implement what she said.

I already mentioned I asked her to clarify her contradictions as I was VERY WELL AWARE she was expecting a rally Wednesday night into March OPEX by sharing a screenshot of a response she gave on X, where she mentioned the Arabs buying. I was aware that she said this because I get push notes from that thread.

But the post yesterday talked about vol down as price would move negatively towards the JPM 4700 strike. Any “rip” or “bounce” was described by her as “vol-up”, “spot-up”, especially in MAG8 names. She also said price was in an oscillating regime.

If she describes a ripping rally as being bad for vol decay, and describes that spot decay (lower SPX) would be good for Vol-decay as price moves towards a 4700, it seems the prospects of a large bounce into March OPEX would be far less likely. How would it not?

I don’t know how else to explain this.

Also, if there is vol decay on rips and we are in an oscillating range, then buying longer term calls in this environment for a March OPEX bounce would seem to be counter productive.

Also, if there is vol decay on rips and we are in an oscillating range, then buying longer term calls in this environment for a March OPEX bounce would seem to be counter productive.

comment.body:
This was not explained in yesterday’s post… seems very logical and reasonable. Don’t know why she kept emphasizing “spot up-vol up”. If she simply said “spot up will also bring volatility down” that would’ve clarified why shorting volatility would make sense… because it would go down either way. She did not say that at all in her commentary yesterday.

Would’ve also been more in line and upped the probabilities for an OPEX bounce. And certainly would give more clarity/confidence to go long because it would still be in line with her short vol trade.

Another thing I find interesting is how she didn’t talk about her “OPEX bounce” at all yesterday, not even mentioning it in her longer term potential outlook about the JPM collar. Didn’t respond to any questions about it either. Then to celebrate it today when she gave completely contradictory analysis yesterday and completely ignored it comes off as a bit disingenuous.

Either way, thanks for your responses. I need to go meditate as this week has ended quite badly. Disappointing not following through with my analysis and intuitions when we have this kind of rally today. There will always be more, but it definitely stings.

And I hate chasing, especially in this environment. But we could easily squeeze another 200 points higher. Guess I gotta find some entries with some tight stops and give it a go. Part of the game. Maybe research a short volatility trade as well 😂.

Have a good weekend Vman/everyone if I don’t talk to you sooner.

comment.body: The tactic isn't "we go down in an oscillating pattern until april". It is oscillating like that because it is a controlled selloff (look at 2022) and not a panic selloff (as seen in SPX/vix beta). As you said, it was obvious there would be a bounce...so why wouldn't you be buying some calls here? Was it because you were concerned on a IV crush? RV is compressing here, short vol funds are going to start pushing the market higher if its enough to overpower the hedging dealer dynamics

comment.body:
Spot up vol up in the context you’re using it in doesn’t seem to correlate to spot up VIX up, but spot up, IV of SPX options up (specifically calls). In negative MM GEX MM’s are buying the rally and selling the dump. 

If traders/customers are buying puts, who’s selling to them? If the MM’s/dealers are selling (shorting) the puts, they need to delta hedge by selling futures because that skews them to a long position, no? 

Not to mention the high negative gamma MM’s have from the JPM collar. Would that not start to play a pretty big roll the closer to expo that gets, adding to the negative gamma environment?


Volume today was pretty sparse, SPX volume was only this low on the way down since Feb 26th where we were also green and then continued down. If HF’s aren’t there like Alma said, that seems indicative to me of a still lack of liquidity. And a precarious place to be. 

Just thinking out loud, we’ll see what next week brings. Price action is doing exactly what I’ve wanted it to do this past week so the theory and thesis I have continues to be present. We shall see though as I have a peasants compared to what Alma is seeing and the info she can digest
